root_text.an9.2:1.1: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an9.2:1.3: Kittāvatā nu kho, bhante, bhikkhu nissayasampanno hotī”ti?
root_text.an9.2:1.4: “Saddhañce, bhikkhu, bhikkhu nissāya akusalaṁ pajahati kusalaṁ bhāveti, pahīnamevassa taṁ akusalaṁ hoti.
root_text.an9.2:1.8: paññañce, bhikkhu, bhikkhu nissāya akusalaṁ pajahati kusalaṁ bhāveti, pahīnamevassa taṁ akusalaṁ hoti.
root_text.an9.2:1.9: Taṁ hissa bhikkhuno akusalaṁ pahīnaṁ hoti suppahīnaṁ, yaṁsa ariyāya paññāya disvā pahīnaṁ.
root_text.an9.2:2.1: Tena ca pana, bhikkhu, bhikkhunā imesu pañcasu dhammesu patiṭṭhāya cattāro upanissāya vihātabbā.
root_text.an9.2:2.3: Idha, bhikkhu, bhikkhu saṅkhāyekaṁ paṭisevati, saṅkhāyekaṁ adhivāseti, saṅkhāyekaṁ parivajjeti, saṅkhāyekaṁ vinodeti.
root_text.an9.2:2.4: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhu, bhikkhu nissayasampanno hotī”ti.

translation_text.an9.2:1.1: Then a mendicant went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.an9.2:1.3: How is a mendicant who is supported defined?”
translation_text.an9.2:1.4: “Mendicant, if a mendicant supported by faith gives up the unskillful and develops the skillful, the unskillful is actually given up by them.
translation_text.an9.2:1.5: If a mendicant supported by conscience …
translation_text.an9.2:1.6: If a mendicant supported by prudence …
translation_text.an9.2:1.7: If a mendicant supported by energy …
translation_text.an9.2:1.8: If a mendicant supported by wisdom gives up the unskillful and develops the skillful, the unskillful is actually given up by them.
translation_text.an9.2:1.9: What’s been given up is completely given up when it has been given up by seeing with noble wisdom.
translation_text.an9.2:2.1: But then, a mendicant grounded on these five things should rely on four things.
translation_text.an9.2:2.3: After appraisal, a mendicant uses some things, endures some things, avoids some things, and gets rid of some things.
translation_text.an9.2:2.4: That’s how a mendicant is supported.”
